title: The Condemned
timelinePosition: Opens hours after Chapter 23's arrest and closes after the verdict with Lor'themar outside the cell.
transport: Static imprisonment -> escorted movement in chains to the tribunal hall -> return to cells -> corridor-side contact through bars.
immediateObjective: Survive the legal machinery long enough to keep the blade intelligence alive while the kingdom misreads the room above them.
constraints: Limited Third Absolute — Sylvanas's perception only
No interior access to Jaina, the magistrates, or late witnesses beyond what Sylvanas can hear, smell, or read from behavior
No cosmic visitor yet — the scale-widening revelation belongs to Chapter 25
No promise of acquittal, prison break, or stable containment; the chapter must let clean legal procedure still produce the wrong answer

povKnowsAtOpen: The pair saved the kingdom in the chamber and still left the room with the worst possible evidence profile.
Jaina is alive but deeply depleted in the adjacent cell.
Frostmourne sits in custody above them, no longer riding through Arthas but still tethered north and still active.
Antonidas reached Capital City before the murder and still missed the room by the same brutal minutes that beat Uther.
Uther arrived too late to witness the murder itself.
Every warning the investigation ever sent can now be reframed as premeditation.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: Whether Antonidas defies Dalaran publicly or obeys the line it draws around the tribunal.
Whether Silvermoon stands behind her or cuts her loose completely.
How quickly Frostmourne is seeking another mind through the palace.
Whether Lor'themar or any outside ally reaches the city in time to affect anything.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No cosmic visitor yet; that belongs to the next chapter's night.
No promise of prison break, acquittal, or even competent containment.
No certainty about who in the palace is already vulnerable to the blade's call until Jaina starts counting the rhythm through stone.
No softening of the legal mechanism into simple corruption; it has to work cleanly and still produce the wrong answer.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: The tribunal convicts both women on every major count and commutes death to indefinite imprisonment.
Antonidas comes with two Kirin Tor magi, but no sanctioned Kirin Tor witness takes the stand.
Uther testifies clearly about Arthas's deterioration and the aftermath and is dismissed as a wounded former commander who did not witness the murder.
Vereesa delivers the Convocation's disavowal in person and stays in the city afterward.
Jaina clocks the blade's acceleration as days rather than weeks.
Lor'themar arrives with three hundred Farstriders and mages who can independently feel the blade's wrongness.

pov.currentStanceAtOpen: The battlefield is gone, but the need to read what is happening has not gone with it.
pov.surfaceObjective: Keep Jaina reachable through stone, keep the truth in circulation as long as possible, and find any remaining tactical surface inside a system built to deny one.
pov.pressureSource: The cold from the vault, Jaina's condition through the wall, the tribunal's elegance, and the certainty that Sylvanas can see each move coming without being able to stop it.
pov.misbeliefManifestation: Some part of Sylvanas still expects clean reading to generate leverage, even in a courtroom designed to leave the accused none.
pov.chapterTest: The chapter asks whether she can endure perfect comprehension without action — the form of helplessness most poisonous to her.
pov.forcedChoice: Keep speaking the truth even as it arms the prosecution, or let silence cede the record entirely.
pov.endStateShift: Sylvanas moves from total abandonment to the first outside reinforcement, but only after the verdict has already locked.
pov.carryForwardResidue: Chapter 25 opens with the pair still imprisoned, the blade seeking faster, and the larger board about to walk through the wall.

throughPov.visibleFunction: Adjacent voice, resonance instrument, and the only person in the palace who can hear how close the blade is to finding another hand.
throughPov.povPressure: Sylvanas reads Jaina through breath, smell, tapping, and the body language of a woman forced to sit near the weapon she already scarred.
throughPov.interactionRule: No interiority. Jaina's pain and calculation stay external.

team.label: partnership
team.operatingModeAtOpen: Post-peak and reduced to what survives stone.
team.operationalChangeThisChapter: The partnership stops being combat choreography and becomes shared endurance plus information transfer.
team.doNotOverstateRule: Let the wall, the silence, and the chain of outside failures prove the bond. Do not overwrite the institutional brutality with sentiment.

entries.rule: Competent, calm, and not secretly villainous. His craft is part of the tragedy.
Elegant prosecutor who uses true facts to build a false causation chain.
Present in the hall, exhausted, furious, and politically muzzled. He gets to be seen and still not get to save her.
Clear, grave, and wounded by history without becoming melodramatic. He can testify to Arthas's deterioration and the aftermath, not to the murder itself.
Sister first in effect even when reading the Convocation's formal words.
Quiet arrival, heavy with cost. He does not promise what the board cannot yet support.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 25 opens at night in the cells with the verdict delivered, the blade accelerating, and the first outside reinforcement still unable to open the door.

scenes.title: The Cells
scenes.sceneFunction: Reframe the triumph as helpless imprisonment and establish Frostmourne as the second, more urgent prison above them.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The room above them proved innocence only to people who were not there to see it.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep stone, cold, bread-smell, and the limited acoustics active so the chapter stays bodily and close.

enrichment.detail: The palace evidence vault sits beneath the tribunal wing, and Frostmourne's unheld state turns containment into ambient pressure: torches gutter, the corridor temperature drops fast, and guards start avoiding the passage before they can explain why. Jaina's cell-distance read should therefore feel like hostile cold and a wrong rhythm through stone, not clean magical clarity.

beats.dilemma: Sylvanas can map the cell and listen to Jaina through the wall, but neither action opens the door or reaches the blade.
beats.decision: She listens anyway because inventory is the only useful discipline left.
newOnPageInformation: The cell is small enough to map in paces and still useless once mapped.
The cold rising through the floor is wrong in direction and intensity, which means the blade is already affecting the building.
Jaina's breath and bread-smell through stone become the only functioning proof that the partnership still exists inside the system.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How quickly the blade's pressure worsens.
Whether the tribunal reads the room as murder, conspiracy, or something even simpler.
sensoryAnchors: thin torch bars on the wall through the grate
frost smell replacing old dungeon damp
Jaina's uneven breathing carrying through masonry to elven ears
beats.transitionIntent: pressure-escalation: once the cells are understood, the legal machine can come inside them.
beats.verifierNotes: Adjacent cells follow Uther's provisional custody and Jaina's necessity as blade-reader; do not imply random prison placement.

beats.goal: Put the investigation's full truth into the official record.
beats.conflict: The truth is too large for the officers taking it down and will read as invention more cleanly than it reads as reality.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas tells the entire truth and the listeners visibly stop believing well before she finishes, which means the transcript itself becomes ammunition.

beats.valueShift: imprisonment understood -> truth enters the wrong record
newOnPageInformation: The prosecution's questions were written before the interrogator entered the cell.
Sylvanas can watch belief fail in real time as the scale of the truth grows.
The entire investigation, stated plainly, sounds like the defense a guilty person would invent if she needed something too large to rebut.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any judge or clerk reads past the implausibility of the content.
Whether Jaina hearing the whole account through the wall helps or only hurts.
sensoryAnchors: clerk's pen scratching quickly across a board balanced on knees
guard-captain posture shifting from attention to endurance
iron lock turning again after the truth is filed away as fantasy
beats.transitionIntent: threat-shift: once the truth is trapped in the wrong record, the blade can announce the deeper problem.

beats.emotion: The law is one prison; Frostmourne is another and hungrier one.
beats.dilemma: The blade is active and looking, but the only people who can interpret what that means are in separate stone boxes.
beats.decision: Sylvanas speaks through the wall and starts treating Jaina's attunement as the only live intelligence source left.
beats.valueShift: truth enters the wrong record -> the blade becomes the urgent clock
newOnPageInformation: The dungeon is getting colder because the blade is pulling heat through the foundations.
Jaina can hear the weapon actively looking for a next mind.
The partnership's wall-language now includes live threat reporting, not just endurance.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Which people in the palace are most vulnerable first.
Whether anyone outside the cells can be made to understand the danger before it chooses a new hand.
sensoryAnchors: breath-clouds thickening in a cell that should not be this cold
Jaina's voice through stone saying one terrible verb: looking
wool blanket on the cot smelling of frost instead of damp
beats.transitionIntent: clock-pressure: once the blade becomes a timetable, the chapter can carry that clock up into the hall.

scenes.title: The Trial
scenes.sceneFunction: Show the system working well enough to condemn the wrong people and let the blade become evidence against its only readers.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The blade is active, the accused are right, and the room above them is built to convert their competence into guilt.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep chains, lead-lined chest, dust-light, and Uther's hands concrete so the trial never drifts into legal abstraction.

enrichment.detail: Capital City is Lordaeron's religious and political heart, so the tribunal should feel ceremonial rather than makeshift: martial-law justice inside a chamber built by monarchy, legitimacy, and Light-centered public order. That setting makes the prosecution's calm certainty more dangerous, because the room itself was designed to make official stories feel true.

beats.goal: Establish the prosecution's narrative and its control of the room.
beats.conflict: The narrative is elegant because it is built from real facts and only reverses the direction of causation.

outcome.summary: Ashcombe builds a clean conspiracy from the investigation's own paperwork, and Sylvanas can see every move while having no way to break the sequence.

beats.valueShift: the blade becomes the urgent clock -> the legal cage closes
newOnPageInformation: The prosecution treats months of correct investigation as months of deliberate preparation.
Scholomance knowledge, Northrend travel, relay work, and warnings all become proof of design instead of response.
Thornton's competence matters because the courtroom is not lawless; it is precise.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any witness can puncture the prosecution's causation chain.
Whether the tribunal sees the pair as traitors, spies, or something worse.
sensoryAnchors: light columns catching courtroom dust
lighter courtroom chains that still say the system already won
two-second look between Jaina and Sylvanas across a floor too wide to cross
beats.transitionIntent: evidence-pressure: once the frame is set, the chapter can bring the blade itself into the room and let fear help that frame.

beats.goal: Present Frostmourne and hear how the court interprets its presence.
beats.conflict: The blade is genuinely horrifying, but the horror is being used to prove the accused knew too much to be innocent.

outcome.summary: The room feels the blade's wrongness, and the prosecution turns that wrongness directly into an argument against the only two people who understand it.

beats.valueShift: the legal cage closes -> the predator enters the courtroom in a box
newOnPageInformation: Even lead, wards, and oak only muffle the blade; they do not quiet it.
Palace mages can describe the weapon's effects without understanding its will or its history.
Jaina is actively harmed by the exhibit while the court mistakes her knowledge for complicity.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: How much faster the vault wards degrade for every minute the blade spends in open hearing space.
Whether anyone in the room notices Jaina's reaction for what it really is.
sensoryAnchors: lead-lined chest on a center table drawing warmth out of the room
fogging breath above the clerk's notes
Jaina gripping chair arms hard enough for the knuckles to whiten
beats.transitionIntent: witness-pressure: once the blade itself is on the floor between them, the chapter can spend Uther's testimony and show why even that is not enough.
beats.verifierNotes: Lead-lined chest and wards are desperate muffling only; do not treat court display as proof Frostmourne is safely contained.

beats.goal: Let Uther give the cleanest truth available to the room.
beats.conflict: The prosecution does not have to prove Uther wrong; it only has to prove him wounded, late to the murder, and therefore interpretable.

outcome.summary: Uther's testimony lands with full factual force and is then neutralized by cross-examination built around Stratholme, his dismissal from command, and his lack of eyewitness standing in the throne room.

beats.valueShift: predator enters courtroom in box -> hope enters and is professionally cut down
newOnPageInformation: Uther can describe Hearthglen, Stratholme, Arthas's deterioration, and the late-arrival aftermath in a way that should dismantle the case.
The court only needs the fact that he was stripped of command and arrived after the act.
Twenty minutes of architectural bad timing become the difference between witness and hearsay.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any later witness is stronger than Uther.
How Jaina is physically holding up under the blade's continued presence.
sensoryAnchors: Uther's hands closing into fists while the prosecutor reframes him
formal paladin surcoat where armor should have been
tribunal room going quieter because everyone feels the witness should matter more than the court lets him matter
beats.transitionIntent: betrayal-pressure: once Uther is cut down, the chapter can move into the deeper institutional failures the verdict still needs.

scenes.title: The Betrayals
scenes.sceneFunction: Let every outside system fail in its own style before the law says the word guilty.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The strongest witness in the kingdom just failed to change the frame.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep Antonidas in the gallery, the witness chair, Vereesa's face, and Thornton's steady procedure concrete so the scene lands through bodies, not only concepts.

enrichment.detail: This scene gains force because three institutions arrive embodied and still fail: Antonidas as head of the Kirin Tor with two magi, Uther as the first paladin and Arthas's old mentor, and Vereesa as the youngest Windrunner sister and former Farstrider. Their titles are load-bearing because the chapter is proving that even the highest legitimate voices in the room cannot bend the verdict fast enough.

beats.emotion: Worse than absence — the mentor arrived and still hit the same wall.
beats.dilemma: Antonidas is in the hall with two Kirin Tor magi, but any sworn testimony or direct blade examination would publicly expose Dalaran's prior warning and failure.
beats.decision: Sylvanas reads the line for what it is and does not waste breath pretending presence is the same thing as help.
beats.valueShift: hope enters and is professionally cut down -> presence without aid
newOnPageInformation: Antonidas came in person and is allowed to observe, object, and ask for continuance, but not to turn himself into official Kirin Tor evidence.
The tribunal treats unsanctioned Kirin Tor examination of Frostmourne as tampering rather than rescue.
The witness chair still stays empty, and that emptiness now lands harder because blue Kirin Tor robes are visible in the room.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Antonidas's conscience breaks later into open defiance.
Whether the tribunal reads his constrained presence as caution, guilt, or political theater.
sensoryAnchors: Antonidas standing in the gallery instead of the witness box
one Kirin Tor mage going pale when the lead-lined chest is opened
Jaina not looking toward her mentor until the objection is denied
beats.transitionIntent: family-pressure: once the mentor is reduced to visible uselessness, the chapter can bring in the sister carrying official severance.

beats.goal: Hear Silvermoon's formal position on Sylvanas in the aftermath of Stratholme and the palace killings.
beats.conflict: The Convocation's words are political, but the voice reading them belongs to Sylvanas's sister.

outcome.summary: The diplomatic shield dissolves completely, and the family bond is made into the instrument that delivers the cut.

beats.valueShift: presence without aid -> family becomes the state's weapon
newOnPageInformation: The Convocation claims Sylvanas acted without authorization and with no sovereign protection behind her.
Vereesa was chosen as courier precisely because the words would hurt more in her voice.
Vereesa stays in the hall afterward, which means the clean narrative is already cracking around the edges.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Vereesa is still acting wholly as the Convocation's instrument by the chapter's end.
Whether any of Silvermoon's political cost can be turned back toward leverage later.
sensoryAnchors: Windrunner crest pinned to the cloak of the woman reading the disavowal
gold wax seal broken in a room full of strangers
one brief look between sisters carrying more than the formal text does
beats.transitionIntent: verdict-pressure: once the state has spoken through family, the verdict can fall with nothing left in the way.
beats.verifierNotes: Vereesa's disavowal is formal Convocation severance, not proof every Silvermoon actor shares the same line.

beats.goal: Hear the tribunal's official answer to everything the investigation did.
beats.conflict: The system is competent, the evidence is real, and the truth exceeds the legal frame offered to hold it.

outcome.summary: Guilty on every major count, death commuted to indefinite imprisonment because politics prefers living prisoners to executed heroines.

beats.valueShift: family becomes the state's weapon -> the wrong answer becomes law
newOnPageInformation: Thornton's ruling follows the prosecution's causation chain all the way to its intended end.
Commutation is political management, not mercy.
The kingdom now has an official history in which its saviors murdered its royals.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether the blade claims another mind before any appeal fiction matters.
Whether any outside force now has enough leverage to reopen what the court just closed.
sensoryAnchors: Thornton's pen steady in the same hand that handled every earlier procedure
Jaina's tremor stopping briefly in shock rather than recovery
guards tightening chains only after the word guilty lands
beats.transitionIntent: threat-return: once the law closes, the chapter can return to the colder problem waiting below it.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.sceneFunction: Make the verdict feel instantly secondary to Frostmourne's acceleration and bring the first outside reinforcement to the door too late.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The verdict is final on paper and useless against the blade above them.
scenes.arcPressure: Keep cell acoustics, tapping, grate-bars, and corridor cadence concrete so the shift from law to countdown feels grounded.

enrichment.detail: Lor'themar's late-arriving Farstriders and Sunwell-raised mages give the chapter its first external corroboration path: where human palace staff describe cold and whispers, elves register the vault as violation. That does not save the verdict, but it turns the blade from private fear into something outside witnesses can finally feel.

beats.emotion: The verdict settles and immediately gets displaced by a colder urgency.
beats.dilemma: They now know the blade's timetable is collapsing, but the only people who know it are still behind locked doors.
beats.decision: Sylvanas starts treating elven sensitivity as the first corroboration path that might exist outside the cells.
beats.valueShift: the wrong answer becomes law -> the real clock reasserts itself
newOnPageInformation: Jaina's tapping through the wall is how she counts the blade's accelerating cycle.
The timeline has shrunk to days, not weeks.
Elven mages near the vault might independently feel enough wrongness to corroborate what Jaina hears precisely.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether any corroboration can move fast enough to beat the blade.
Which palace minds are already nearest the call.
sensoryAnchors: fingertip taps through stone counting the blade's heartbeat
air in the cell getting colder after the verdict instead of warmer
Sylvanas holding a possible plan warm in her head because that is all she can physically hold
beats.transitionIntent: arrival-pressure: once the corroboration thought exists, the chapter can bring Lor'themar to the grate and give it an outside surface.

beats.goal: Bring Lor'themar to the cell and establish what outside force is actually in play now.
beats.conflict: He arrived after the verdict, which means his presence changes the landscape but cannot undo what just happened.

outcome.summary: Lor'themar brings three hundred Farstriders and mages who already felt the blade in the palace foundations, but the cell remains shut and the blade's timeline remains brutally short.

beats.valueShift: the real clock reasserts itself -> reinforcement without release
newOnPageInformation: Lor'themar won just enough in Silvermoon to move a real force into Capital City.
The elven mages can independently feel the vault's wrongness from outside the cells.
Sylvanas is no longer alone politically, only still trapped physically.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Lor'themar can turn perception into leverage before the blade chooses.
Whether Vereesa's continued presence helps or hinders that move.
sensoryAnchors: familiar Farstrider cadence in dungeon footsteps before the face appears
road-dust on Lor'themar where court polish should be
two hands meeting across cold iron bars for the only contact the chapter allows
beats.transitionIntent: handoff-pressure: Chapter 25 opens at night with the cells still closed, the blade still seeking, and the larger board about to show one corner of itself.
beats.verifierNotes: Lor'themar's force is a late, separate political move, not a reversal of the Convocation's public severance or of the locked verdict.

warnings: Beats 1-5: the chapter darkens in one direction from cell inventory through the prosecution's framing. Differentiate imprisonment, transcript damage, blade-clock pressure, and courtroom control sharply or the opening half will flatten into one long descent.

Beats 4-9: the tribunal core runs six consecutive scene beats without sequel relief. Keep chains, witness chair, lead chest, Vereesa's document, and Thornton's procedure physically active so the middle does not drift into abstract legal summary.

Scene-beat outcome variety is narrow: beats 2, 4, 5, 6, 8, and 9 all land no-and, with beat 11 the lone yes-but. The prose will need strong cost differentiation so every loss changes shape instead of repeating.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter23's chamber custody: after Uther takes Jaina and Sylvanas under his authority in the Imperial Chamber, the palace converts emergency control into dungeon procedure, hauls Frostmourne into an improvised lead-and-warded evidence vault above the cells, and keeps the two women adjacent because Jaina is too dangerous to separate cleanly from the blade she can still hear while Sylvanas remains part of the same damning room-story. By chapter24's opening, the transfer has already taught the building the wrong cold and turned chamber aftermath into formal imprisonment.

craftResearch.findings: Open in present pressure rather than recap; use immediate sensory confinement, bodily movement, and a live story question so aftermath chapters begin with momentum instead of summary.
craftResearch.source: CRAFT; The History Quill; TCK Publishing

craftResearch.findings: Anchor procedure in character stakes, let testimony change pressure rather than summarize facts, and keep body language and witness vulnerability concrete so the room stays dramatic instead of abstract.

Differentiate each setback by function and emotional shape, vary conflict types, and interleave short reflective beats with escalating external pressure so repeated losses still feel like forward motion.
craftResearch.source: Helping Writers Become Authors; Writer's Digest; The Book Coach

craftResearch.findings: Let the formal loss stand, then end on unresolved consequence, emotional residue, and the first hint of next action so defeat hooks the next chapter instead of closing it down.

pacing.notes: Beat 1 opens in active confinement rather than recap, beats 2-3 turn imprisonment into a double clock of law and blade, beats 4-6 keep the tribunal changing shape through frame, exhibit, and witness failure, beats 7-9 vary the losses through mentor, family, and verdict, and beats 10-11 give the chapter the necessary late pivot from legal defeat to looming magical countdown.

structure.notes: The chapter has a clean four-scene descent: cells, tribunal, institutional betrayals, then aftermath. The midchapter turn lands when Frostmourne enters the room and Uther still cannot break the frame, while the close stays load-bearing because the guilty verdict is immediately displaced by the sharper problem of a still-seeking blade and too-late outside reinforcement.

character.notes: Sylvanas remains the strict perceptual owner of every beat, Jaina stays external through breath, posture, tapping, and visible strain, and the POV character still exercises agency in beats 1, 2, 3, 7, and 10 even inside captivity. Supporting functions stay distinct: Thornton is lawful procedure, Ashcombe false causation, Uther moral truth without evidentiary force, Antonidas political impotence, Vereesa official family wound, and Lor'themar late reinforcement.

transitions.notes: All 11 beat-level transitionIntent links were checked for causal coverage and clean scene-boundary landing. The cell opening now carries explicitly from chapter23 through a written chapterBridge, the trial-to-cells return preserves the blade clock without a dropped thread, and the final handoff aligns with chapter25's prison-night opening.

continuity.notes: 0 new beats, 0 callbacks, and 11 evolutions: chapter24 is almost entirely fallout from chapter23's chamber victory, custody crisis, and active-blade aftermath. No contradictions were found in Sylvanas's wounded command-state, Jaina's depletion, Uther's lateness, Antonidas's political limits, or the palace evidence profile; anti-reintroduction guards were added for custody placement, inadequate Frostmourne containment, and Silvermoon's split public versus extra-procedural response.

modifications.description: Annotated every beat with continuityStatus, added beat-level memoryRef entries for inherited fallout, and assembled the requiredMemory reading list around the two chapter23 aftermath threads plus the key character and relationship files.

Added a direct bridge from chapter23's Imperial Chamber custody into the dungeon opening, clarifying the transfer into adjacent cells and the improvised movement of Frostmourne into a nearby evidence vault.

Added verifierNotes to prevent false continuity drift around prison placement, the court's inadequate handling of Frostmourne, and the apparent contradiction between Vereesa's disavowal and Lor'themar's later arrival.
